parts.content:
Write 1 dissimilar SBA question(s) on: 'The resting membrane potential is determined mainly by potassium permeability.'.

Each question should test application of knowledge, not simple recall — a candidate should need to reason from principles rather than just retrieve a fact. Use the retrieve tool to find relevant reference material before writing. Return the result as a QuestionSet.

parts.args: {"query": 'The resting membrane potential is determined mainly by potassium permeability.'}

{"topic":"Primary Frca Sample Sbas","exam":"primary","curriculum_node_code":null,"model":"openai:gpt-5.5","generated_at":"2026-05-02T01:16:45.156172","questions":[{"stem":"In an experimental situation, a myelinated axon is bathed in an electrolyte solution containing chloride, potassium, and sodium ions.","lead":"Which change would have the greatest effect in making the resting membrane potential less negative?","options":[{"text":"Decreasing the extracellular concentration of potassium ions","is_correct":false,"explanation":"Reducing extracellular potassium increases the potassium gradient out of the cell and tends to hyperpolarise the membrane."},{"text":"Decreasing the extracellular concentration of sodium ions","is_correct":false,"explanation":"The resting membrane is relatively impermeable to sodium, so changing extracellular sodium has relatively little effect on resting membrane potential."},{"text":"Increasing the extracellular concentration of chloride ions","is_correct":false,"explanation":"Chloride permeability is not the dominant determinant of resting membrane potential in axons compared with potassium permeability."},{"text":"Increasing the extracellular concentration of potassium ions","is_correct":true,"explanation":"Increasing extracellular potassium reduces the potassium concentration gradient, making the potassium equilibrium potential less negative and depolarising the resting membrane potential."},{"text":"Increasing the extracellular concentration of sodium ions","is_correct":false,"explanation":"Although sodium has an inward electrochemical gradient, the resting membrane has low sodium permeability, so this has less effect than changing potassium."}],"explanation":"The resting membrane potential is determined mainly by potassium permeability. Increasing extracellular potassium reduces potassium efflux and makes the membrane potential less negative, as predicted by the Nernst and Goldman equations.","curriculum_node_codes":[],"curriculum_node_scores":[]}]}
